text_blocks.text: on 17 February 1546. After 8 a.m., he experienced chest
pains. When he went to his bed, he prayed, "Into your
hand I commit my spirit; you have redeemed me, O Lord,
faithful God" (Ps. 31.5), the common prayer of the dying
At 1 am, he awake with more chest pain and was
warmed with hot vowels. He thanked God for revealing
his Son to him in whom he had believed. His
companions, Justus Jonas and Michael Coelius, shouted
loudly, "Reverend father, are you ready to die trusting in
your Lord Jesus Christ and to confess the doctrine
which you have taught in his name? A distinct "Yes" was
